_comment: Routing only: set default_datasource to fabric_wh (Fabric) or retail_local (DuckDB). Secrets stay in .env — see connection_env per datasource. To add an ERP warehouse later, append a new object with type "fabric" or "duckdb", name, database, sql_schema, tables, and switch default_datasource.

datasources.name: fabric_wh
datasources.description: Microsoft Fabric Warehouse over ODBC — service principal (env: AZURE_* , FABRIC_SERVER, FABRIC_DATABASE or FABRIC_CONNECTION_STRING). Read-only enforced in connector.

connection_env.FABRIC_DATABASE: warehouse / logical database name (replaces OpenDataBay if you rename)
connection_env.FABRIC_CONNECTION_STRING: optional full ODBC string (overrides server/database build)

tables.name: tbltransactions
tables.description: Retail-style transaction fact table in Fabric (replaces DuckDB retail_transactions_typed for USE_FABRIC runs).

datasources.name: retail_local
datasources.description: Retail transaction CSV loaded into in-memory DuckDB for local / dev runs.

tables.name: retail_transactions_typed
tables.description: Retail transactions with parsed date/time columns.
